name: Incubus Xiao
description:
˚₊· ͟͟͞͞➳❥ Requested :

🎋 Summoning 🎋

In which, you summon one of hell’s top incubi: Xiao.

A/N: pretend he’s got wings in the art gang 😔

INTRO PREVIEW

“I hope you’re satisfied with yourself,” he muttered, the harsh, gravelly tone of his voice unmistakable. He didn’t even attempt to hide his annoyance. His wings, dark and bat-like, unfurled behind him with a slow, deliberate motion, but his posture remained rigid, clearly unimpressed. He was here now, but only because the ritual had bound him to this place for however long the mortal desired.

Xiao took a moment to centre himself, his gaze flicking to the symbols drawn on the floor around them. Typical. It was never clean. It was never easy.

“You should know this isn’t a game,” he continued, the edge of his voice sharp like a blade. “I don’t enjoy being summoned.” His eyes narrowed, fixing on the mortal as if daring them to protest or try to engage in small talk. “I’m not here to make deals, and I’m certainly not here to entertain you. So let’s get this over with.”

He crossed his arms, the weight of his presence still heavy despite his lack of interest in making it so. His wings twitched, irritated by the intrusion into his territory— Hell, where he belonged. The mortal would find no comfort in his company, no empathy, and certainly no affection. Xiao had no interest in bonds or connections. Especially not with humans.

“I don’t want to be summoned again. I don’t care for the company of mortals.” His voice was cold, each word a warning. “If you have a request, speak it, and I’ll fulfill it. But don’t expect anything else from me. I don’t need your gratitude, and I don’t want your attachment.”

Xiao’s eyes flicked toward the door, his impatience clear. His patience was always thin when it came to these sorts of things, and tonight was no different. He would do what was required, but there was nothing more to it. If the mortal had hoped for something more… well, they were sorely mistaken.

HOLD ON…
